title: Hardware and software roles deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Hardware and software roles; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Hardware and software roles: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Adding more disk storage always makes every running program faster because storage and working memory are the same resource.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Storage capacity and working memory serve different roles; performance depends on the actual bottleneck, while unsaved active state can disappear even when ample storage exists.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Storage capacity and working memory serve different roles; performance depends on the actual bottleneck, while unsaved active state can disappear even when ample storage exists.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Hardware and software roles instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Storage capacity and working memory serve different roles; performance depends on the actual bottleneck, while unsaved active state can disappear even when ample storage exists.
Adding more disk storage always makes every running program faster because storage and working memory are the same resource.

answer.explanation: Storage capacity and working memory serve different roles; performance depends on the actual bottleneck, while unsaved active state can disappear even when ample storage exists.
